en.extract:
The glistening-green tanager (Chlorochrysa phoenicotis) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is found in Colombia and Ecuador.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.
Males are almost entirely bright glistening emerald green, with small gray patches behind and below the eye with another on the shoulders.  Females are slightly duller than males.

The '''glistening-green tanager''' ('''''Chlorochrysa phoenicotis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Thraupidae]]. It is found in [[Colombia]] and [[Ecuador]].

Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

Males are almost entirely bright glistening emerald green, with small gray patches behind and below the eye with another on the shoulders.  Females are slightly duller than males.<ref>Ridgely, Robert and Paul Greenfield. The Birds of Ecuador. Volume II: Field Guide. Cornell, 2001</ref>
